author.bio: A lifestyle brand offering timeless products of unique modernity. @ipfsbot at @protocollabs
author.name: dietrich

content.text:
Actually, the IPFS team engaged @igalia for this work, to accelerate browser features needed by all dweb protocols. 

And get Chromium up to par with Gecko in this case. Compat matters!

No signal if Google will ship it in Chrome, but other Chromium-based browsers are interested.
content.html:
Actually, the IPFS team engaged @igalia for this work, to accelerate browser features needed by all dweb protocols.

And get Chromium up to par with Gecko in this case. Compat matters!

No signal if Google will ship it in Chrome, but other Chromium-based browsers are interested.

author.bio:
Senior editor, CNET News. I cover browsers, photography, lots of new tech. DMs open or email first.last@cbsi.com

The squirming facts exceed the squamous mind.
author.name: Stephen Shankland

content.text: Google plans to add into Chrome the ability to handle decentralized web protocols (e.g. dat, dweb, ipfs, ipns, ssb). Decentralized web = more of a peer-to-peer design, where individuals host their own content. https://groups.google.com/a/chromium.org/forum/#!topic/blink-dev/7nHTRUP1EGY
content.html: Google plans to add into Chrome the ability to handle decentralized web protocols (e.g. dat, dweb, ipfs, ipns, ssb).
